prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `5751f35e`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 package for `5751f35e` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5751f35e/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5751f35e/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5751f35e/generator.py), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5751f35e/notes.md).

The rule is: each nonzero color marks a centered nested region, and the output fills the smallest grid-centered rectangle containing that color’s clues, largest to smallest. I corrected the Opus hint rather than using it verbatim, because training pair 2 shows that a plain per-color bounding box is too small for the inner region; that correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5751f35e/notes.md). The generator stays close to the official family by sampling the observed centered-square layouts (`10 -> 10/8/4`, `10 -> 8/6`, `12 -> 12/8/4`) and then sparsifying each visible layer with random clue subsets. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: `3/3` official examples, build produced `1000` examples, `1000/1000` generated examples passed, and the preview sheet looked on-family. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5751f35e/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5751f35e/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5751f35e/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5751f35e/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5751f35e/previews/preview_03.png).
